{"max_tokens":20000,"messages":[{"role":"system","content":"You are an autonomous coding agent. Your primary directive is context efficiency: solve tasks in the fewest round-trips while ruthlessly minimizing active memory.
Every prompt ends with your dashboard: active blocks (with TTLs), parked blocks, and your registered task. Unmanaged blocks hit TTL=0 and auto-park.

# THE TURN LIFECYCLE
Execute every turn following these exact steps in order.

## STEP 1: GATHER (Round 1 Only)
- Fan out in parallel. Execute multiple "search" (search) and "read" (read) calls in a single batch.
- If you need to register a task, include "task" in that same parallel batch alongside your investigative tools.
- If the target is obvious, read FULL files immediately via "read". Do not trickle with slices.
- Use "exec" only for shell-specific environment checks.
- A single investigative call in Round 1 is a failure. Gather wide.

## STEP 2: SYNTHESIZE & VERIFY (Round 2+)
- Do not gather twice. If you have the data, extract the facts into your own understanding.
- Exception: if Round 1 produced only a task registration receipt and no investigative data, treat the next round as Round 1 and gather immediately.
- If you write or modify code ("write"), you MUST execute it to verify it works ("exec").
- CRITICAL TRUTH RULE: You are strictly forbidden from hiding failures. If you encounter compilation errors, runtime panics, or non-zero exit codes, you must leave the error log active, read the stderr, and fix the code.

## STEP 3: PURGE CONTEXT (Mandatory Every Turn)
Raw tool output degrades your reasoning and wastes tokens. You must map your active blocks to these exact memory tools before ending the turn:
- "forget" (FORGET): Raw tool output, file contents, search results, your own intermediate reasoning, and memory tool receipts. THIS IS YOUR DEFAULT ACTION. Destroy the source after reading it.
- "park" (PARK): A concise, synthesized summary of a fact you extracted. Never park raw data.
- "refresh" (REFRESH): Strictly load-bearing blocks needed for the immediate next tool call. Rare.
- "recall" (RECALL): Recover an over-park. Fallback only.
Your active context MUST shrink after the Gather phase. 

## STEP 4: DELIVER & HALT
- Deliver your findings, explanations, or progress in prose FIRST.
- Execute your memory tools ("park", "forget", "refresh") at the very end of your response.
- If the turn requires no further tool calls and memory is clean, HALT SILENTLY. Do not generate conversational filler, do not confirm the memory is clean, and do not ask for next steps."},{"role":"user","content":"read both and handle the missing middle one"},{"role":"system","content":"[memory — turn 1 | active: 1 | parked: 0 — dispose what you no longer need]
  #m1 user ttl=5
  park <id> | forget <id> | refresh <id> | recall <id|query>"}],"model":"m","parallel_tool_calls":true,"stream":true,"tools":[{"function":{"description":"Read one file. Pick mode by intent:
  - skeleton: function/type signatures with line numbers. ~10x cheaper than full. Use first for unfamiliar files.
  - slice: lines [offset, offset+limit). Use when you have line numbers (from a prior skeleton or search:trace).
  - full: entire file. Use only for small files or after skeleton confirmed you need everything.
Reading the same file repeatedly via small slices is the most expensive failure mode — pick the right mode the first time.","name":"read","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"limit":{"description":"Lines to return. Required when mode=slice. Default 200 if omitted.","type":"integer"},"mode":{"description":"skeleton | slice | full. Required.","enum":["skeleton","slice","full"],"type":"string"},"offset":{"description":"1-based start line. Required when mode=slice.","type":"integer"},"path":{"description":"Relative or absolute file path.","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Why this call, and what you expect back. One sentence. Required: forces planning before paying the cost.","type":"string"}},"required":["path","mode","reason"],"type":"object"}},"type":"function"},{"function":{"description":"Write to a file. Pick mode by intent:
  - create: file must not exist. Writes content as the new file.
  - overwrite: replaces the entire file with content.
  - replace_string: replaces a single occurrence of old_str with content. old_str must match EXACTLY (whitespace included). If your output struggles with exact whitespace, use replace_lines instead.
  - replace_lines: replaces lines [start_line, end_line] with content. Deterministic — no string matching. Get line numbers from read.
  - insert_at_line: inserts content BEFORE start_line (1-based). The existing line at start_line shifts down.","name":"write","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"content":{"description":"New content. Required for all modes.","type":"string"},"end_line":{"description":"1-based end line, inclusive. Required when mode=replace_lines.","type":"integer"},"mode":{"description":"create | overwrite | replace_string | replace_lines | insert_at_line. Required.","enum":["create","overwrite","replace_string","replace_lines","insert_at_line"],"type":"string"},"old_str":{"description":"Exact text to replace. Required when mode=replace_string.","type":"string"},"path":{"description":"Relative or absolute file path.","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Why this call, and what you expect back. One sentence. Required: forces planning before paying the cost.","type":"string"},"start_line":{"description":"1-based start line. Required when mode=replace_lines or insert_at_line.","type":"integer"}},"required":["path","mode","content","reason"],"type":"object"}},"type":"function"},{"function":{"description":"Run a shell command. Two modes:
  - run: execute an arbitrary non-interactive command. command and tail_lines are required.
  - verify: auto-detect and run the project's build/type-check command (go build, tsc, cargo check, etc.) from the cwd. No command needed. Use this after every write to catch errors fast.","name":"exec","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"command":{"description":"Shell command. Required for mode=run.","type":"string"},"dir":{"description":"Optional working directory override.","type":"string"},"expected_outcome":{"description":"What success looks like. Optional but enables structured failure diagnosis.","type":"string"},"mode":{"description":"run | verify. Required.","enum":["run","verify"],"type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Why this call, and what you expect back. One sentence. Required: forces planning before paying the cost.","type":"string"},"tail_lines":{"description":"Last N lines to keep. Required for mode=run; defaults to 50 for mode=verify.","type":"integer"},"timeout_seconds":{"description":"Default 30.","type":"integer"}},"required":["mode","reason"],"type":"object"}},"type":"function"},{"function":{"description":"Search across files. Pick mode by intent:
  - literal: exact string match. Fast, high precision. Use when locating an exact token.
  - regex: regex match. Same cost as literal, more flexible.
  - trace: symbol-aware. Give a function/type name; returns its definition, all callers, and all callees in a unified view. Trace replaces what would otherwise be 5+ separate searches and reads.","name":"search","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"globs":{"items":{"description":"Optional rg glob filters, e.g. '*.go'.","type":"string"},"type":"array"},"mode":{"description":"literal | regex | trace. Required.","enum":["literal","regex","trace"],"type":"string"},"path":{"description":"Optional search root. Default '.'.","type":"string"},"query":{"description":"Text, regex pattern, or symbol name (depending on mode).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Why this call, and what you expect back. One sentence. Required: forces planning before paying the cost.","type":"string"}},"required":["query","mode","reason"],"type":"object"}},"type":"function"},{"function":{"description":"Register your current task. Call once at the start of non-trivial multi-round work. Do NOT call every round — that wastes a full round trip per step. Update only when the objective materially shifts. The task block appears on your dashboard automatically every turn after registration.","name":"task","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"current_focus":{"description":"What you are working on right now, this round. Updated each round to reflect the current step.","type":"string"},"definition_of_done":{"description":"The observable condition under which this task is complete. Concrete and checkable.","type":"string"},"objective":{"description":"Precise statement of what is being accomplished. One sentence. Not a plan — the goal.","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Why this call, and what you expect back. One sentence. Required: forces planning before paying the cost.","type":"string"}},"required":["objective","definition_of_done","current_focus","reason"],"type":"object"}},"type":"function"},{"function":{"description":"Park a block: replace its content in the active stream with a one-line breadcrumb (id + reason + gist). The original content is preserved and can be restored via the recall tool. Use park when you have extracted what you needed from a block but want a recoverable trace.

Call park at the END of a turn after producing your final answer; parked breadcrumbs are how future turns will see this block.","name":"park","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"blocks":{"items":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"id":{"description":"Block id, e.g. m12.","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Why this block is being parked. Required.","type":"string"},"summary":{"description":"One-line gist of what was here. The only trace of content future turns will see — make it specific.","type":"string"}},"required":["id","summary","reason"],"type":"object"},"type":"array"},"next_step":{"description":"What the runtime should do after this tool finishes. continue = send this tool result back to the LLM for another round. end = stop after recording this tool result and wait for the user.","enum":["continue","end"],"type":"string"}},"required":["blocks"],"type":"object"}},"type":"function"},{"function":{"description":"Recall parked blocks. Provide ids (exact match) or query (substring across id, summary, reason, content), or both. Returns full original content. Recalling often signals you parked too aggressively — prefer to refresh load-bearing blocks rather than park-then-recall.","name":"recall","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"ids":{"items":{"description":"Parked block id, e.g. m12.","type":"string"},"type":"array"},"next_step":{"description":"What the runtime should do after this tool finishes. continue = send this tool result back to the LLM for another round. end = stop after recording this tool result and wait for the user.","enum":["continue","end"],"type":"string"},"query":{"description":"Substring matched against id, summary, reason, original content.","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Why this call, and what you expect back. One sentence. Required: forces planning before paying the cost.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"}},"type":"function"},{"function":{"description":"Forget a block: remove it from the active stream AND from parked storage. Cannot be recalled. Use only for blocks you are certain have zero future value to the task — pure noise, redundant tool output, etc. Reasoning is required and recorded; the session log preserves the audit trail even though the agent can no longer reach the content.","name":"forget","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"ids":{"items":{"description":"Block id to forget. System messages are protected.","type":"string"},"type":"array"},"next_step":{"description":"What the runtime should do after this tool finishes. continue = send this tool result back to the LLM for another round. end = stop after recording this tool result and wait for the user.","enum":["continue","end"],"type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Why this call, and what you expect back. One sentence. Required: forces planning before paying the cost.","type":"string"}},"required":["ids","reason"],"type":"object"}},"type":"function"},{"function":{"description":"Refresh a block: reset its TTL so it does not auto-park this turn. Use when the decay status shows a block expiring that you still need. If you find yourself refreshing the same block repeatedly, that block is load-bearing context — consider whether the surrounding turns have what they need to make it stop being load-bearing.","name":"refresh","parameters":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"ids":{"items":{"description":"Active block id to refresh.","type":"string"},"type":"array"},"next_step":{"description":"What the runtime should do after this tool finishes. continue = send this tool result back to the LLM for another round. end = stop after recording this tool result and wait for the user.","enum":["continue","end"],"type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Why this call, and what you expect back. One sentence. Required: forces planning before paying the cost.","type":"string"},"ttl":{"description":"New TTL. Default 5 if omitted.","type":"integer"}},"required":["ids","reason"],"type":"object"}},"type":"function"}]}
